introduction.p: A potato initially at room temperature (68^\circ) is placed in an oven (at 350^\circ) at time t = 0. It is known that the potato's temperature at time t is given by the function F(t) = a - b(0.98)^t for some positive constants a and b, where F is measured in degrees Fahrenheit and t is time in minutes.

statement.p: What is the numerical value of F(0)? What does this tell you about the value of a - b?

solution.p: The numerical value of F(0) is 68^\circ, because the potato begins at room temperature. Therefore, F(0) = a - b(0.98)^0 = a - b = 68.

statement.p: Based on the context of the problem, what should be the long-range behavior of the function F(t)? Use this fact along with the behavior of (0.98)^t to determine the value of a. Write a sentence to explain your thinking.

solution.p: The long-range behavior of F(t) should reflect the potato approaching the oven temperature of 350^\circ. The expression (0.98)^t approaches zero as t gets large, so F(t) \to a. Therefore a = 350^\circ.

statement.p: What is the value of b? Why?

solution.p: The value of b is a - 68 = 350 - 68 = 282^\circ, since we established that a - b = 68 and a = 350.

statement.p: Check your work above by plotting the function F using graphing technology in an appropriate window. Record your results on the axes provided below, labeling the scale on the axes. Then, use the graph to estimate the time at which the potato's temperature reaches 325 degrees.

description.p: ADD ALT TEXT TO THIS IMAGE

solution.p: The function F(t) = 350 - 282(0.98)^t is shown below. The potato reaches 325 degrees after about 120 minutes of baking in the 350 degree oven.

statement.p: How can we view the function F(t) = a - b(0.98)^t as a transformation of the parent function f(t) = (0.98)^t? Explain.

solution.p: We can view F(t) = a - b(0.98)^t as a transformation of the parent function f(t) = (0.98)^t by first stretching f vertically by a factor of b, then reflecting over the t-axis, and finally shifting vertically up by a units.
